title: Unable to build container in container with storage vfs
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Deploy AWX in docker container 
2. Install podman (with dnf install)
3. clone https://github.com/openshift/golang-ex
4. try to build golang-ex


**Describe the results you received:**

I have a permission error : 

```
(home) bash-4.4# podman build . -t registry.home.lan/dev/golang-ex
STEP 1: FROM golang:1.11
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob bbab4ec87ac4 done  
Copying blob 4ae16bd47783 done  
Copying blob 2ea1f7804402 done  
Copying blob 96465440c208 done  
Copying blob 16a3d8aca6cd done  
Copying blob 3a62db25bdbe done  
Copying blob 8c47c6b16c60 done  
Copying config 43a154fee7 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: USER nobody
--> c08556a5fc6
STEP 3: RUN mkdir -p /go/src/github.com/openshift/golang-ex
error running container: error creating new mount namespace for [/bin/sh -c mkdir -p /go/src/github.com/openshift/golang-ex]: operation 
not permitted
Error: error building at STEP "RUN mkdir -p /go/src/github.com/openshift/golang-ex": error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
The container should be build

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.15.1-1.el8.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
(home) bash-4.4# buildah version
Version:         1.15.1
Go Version:      go1.13.4
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.5.1
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
(home) bash-4.4# podman version
Version:      2.0.3
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.13.4
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
CentOS Linux release 8.2.2004 (Core) 
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux awx 3.10.0-1127.13.1.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Tue Jun 23 15:46:38 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
[storage]
driver = "vfs"
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
]
size = ""
override_kernel_check = "true"
[storage.options.thinpool]
ostree_repo = ""
skip_mount_home = "false"
```

This worked for me with buildah bud
```
golang-ex (master) $ buildah bud -t registry.home.lan/dev/golang-ex .
STEP 1: FROM golang:1.11
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob bbab4ec87ac4 done

Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: USER nobody
STEP 3: RUN mkdir -p /go/src/github.com/openshift/golang-ex
STEP 4: WORKDIR /go/src/github.com/openshift/golang-ex
STEP 5: COPY . /go/src/github.com/openshift/golang-ex
STEP 6: RUN go build
go: disabling cache (/nonexistent/.cache/go-build) due to initialization failure: mkdir /nonexistent: permission denied
STEP 7: CMD ["./golang-ex"]
STEP 8: COMMIT registry.home.lan/dev/golang-ex
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 660314270d76 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 6d5a64ea8f37 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 74e2ede3b29c skipped: already exists

buildah version 1.15.0 (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)
```

--> 377e761ef8a
STEP 7: CMD ["./golang-ex"]
STEP 8: COMMIT registry.home.lan/dev/golang-ex
--> 4d33d53387f
4d33d53387f88d334999b82d32325e25f167f544bae4acd3bcebe32cd201a1d3
golang-ex (master) $ podman --version
podman version 2.1.0-dev
```

@rhatdan Thank you.
Are you building from AWX tasks container or another container ?

with buildah I have this error :

```
(home) bash-4.4# buildah bud -t registry.home.lan/dev/golang-ex .
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Invalid argument
User namespaces are not enabled in /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces.
ERRO error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax 
ERRO (unable to determine exit status)            
(home) bash-4.4# 
```

Well I did not use the vfs storage, which would be one difference, that I am just now noticing?  I use overlay (fuse-overlayfs to be exact)
Any reason  you are using vfs?

Are you doing this build as root or non root?  I did my tests as non-root. 

I also did
$ git clone git@github.com:openshift/golang-ex.git
$ cd golang-ex
$ buildah bud  -t registry.home.lan/dev/golang-ex .

I am using vfs, because with overlay my playbook fails.
I am root on my container :

`uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)`

I can see I have no user namespace available : 

```
(home) bash-4.4# sysctl -A | grep namespace
user.max_ipc_namespaces = 127848
user.max_mnt_namespaces = 127848
user.max_net_namespaces = 127848
user.max_pid_namespaces = 127848
user.max_user_namespaces = 0
user.max_uts_namespaces = 127848
(home) bash-4.4# sysctl user.max_user_namespaces=1500
sysctl: setting key "user.max_user_namespaces": Read-only file system
(home) bash-4.4# sudo bash -c 'echo 10000 > /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces'
bash: /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces: Read-only file system

```

> Well I did not use the vfs storage, which would be one difference, that I am just now noticing? I use overlay (fuse-overlayfs to be exact)
> Any reason you are using vfs?
> 
> Are you doing this build as root or non root? I did my tests as non-root.
> 
> I also did
> $ git clone [git@github.com](mailto:git@github.com):openshift/golang-ex.git
> $ cd golang-ex
> $ buildah bud -t registry.home.lan/dev/golang-ex .

With overlay I've got this error : 

`[root@64ec7f5be887 golang-ex]# podman build . -t golang-ex
Error: could not get runtime: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
[root@64ec7f5be887 golang-ex]# 
`

You need to install fuse-overlay on your system and then reset your storage.
This will allow you to use rootless overlay.

@rhatdan : can you run this command and show me the result :

`sysctl -A | grep namespace`

```
# sysctl -A | grep namespace

I had to reinstall my server. I am now on Centos 8.2 and I will try to use fuse-overlayfs.

but in awx container I've got this error :

> (home) bash-4.4# podman build . -t golang-ex
> Error: could not get runtime: operation not permitted

This is my configuration : 





                            +-------------------server+
                            |                         |
                            |                         |          containers
                            |   +----------awx_task   |             +
    I am trying to run      |   |                |    |             |
    podman in awx_task--------------+          v--------------------+
    container, to build     |   +----------------+    |             |
    golang-ex               |                         |             |
                            |   +---------awx_web+    |             |
                            |   |                |    |             |

                            |                         |             |
                            |   +---------redis--+    |             |
                            |   |                |    |             |

                            |   +----------------+    |             |
                            |                         |             |
                            |   +----------postgres   |             |
                            |   |                |    |             |
                            |   |               v-------------------+
                            |   +----------------+    |
                            |                         |
                            |                   v-------------+  docker runtine
                            +-------------------------+


I have a baremetal server named server and I am running 4 containers with docker as runtime (with docker-compose).
I want to run podman from awx_task.
Running a container within a container is not supported without running a privileged container.

**server** is a baremetal server running awx containers.
in awx_task I don't want to run a container. I just want to build a container and push the image in a registry.
Only way to do this is with buildah directly and --isolation=chroot.
